entry.title: SAP Certification
entry.content:
I am writing this post for two main reasons. This is with regard to the SAP Certification exams held in India.

  1. Based on my personal experience, I would like to warn all potential SAP certification candidates to be careful when applying for the exam in India. I attended a 4-month course and appeared for the SAP-Certified Associate-Back-end-Developer-ABAP Cloud exam on 30th May 2024. I had also studied using a sample paper after double and cross-checking all the answers from trustworthy sources including the SAP help portal and SAP community. So I was very well-versed in the questions and answers of the sample paper which was the exact same paper set by the SAP certification board. So imagine my shock when I got only 56% in the exam which I was sure to pass with a minimum percentage of 90% or more. When I asked them to show me my answer sheet and how they graded it, there was just deathly silence at their end. Still awaiting their response. Please note that the exam fees are quite expensive for many who just want to get a job or a promotion to take care of their families. I would also like to inform the candidates that the proctors are clueless on how to guide a candidate using IOS.
  2. The second important reason for posting this is to request whoever is globally responsible for SAP Certifications to please make the whole process transparent and ethical.

entry.title: Preliminary What’s New Information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402.4
entry.content:
Preliminary versions of What’s New information and of the Product Assistance are now available for customers and partners to get a preview on the upcoming new and changed features of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402.4.

You can find this preview on the current Help Portal page SAP S/4HANA Cloud (see What’s New section “Preview of SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2402.4”). To access the Preliminary What’s New information, please use the What’s New Viewer or the Release Assessment and Scope Dependency (RASD) tool.

The RASD tool provides you with a personalized and tailored view on What’s New information based on the activated and used scope within your system. The preliminary information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2402.4 will be available in RASD within the next few days. We recommend using RASD to prepare for upgrades and updates of your system and to focus on the new and changed features that are relevant for you.

A valid S-User ID is needed to use the RASD tool and to view all the preliminary content stated above.

entry.title: Basics of Nodejs and Expressjs in SAP BAS
entry.content:
A very basic of initialising node and express in SAP BAS project.

  1. Create a full stack dev space. Lets call it “node”.

 

2. Open the dev space. Create empty folder “nodebasics1” in workspace.

3. Right click and open integrated terminal.

 

4. Run the first command “npm init”. This is the very first command to be executed when setting up any node project.

 

5. After executing command, it will ask few details on package name, licence, description, author, entry point(starting file), etc. Provide the details if required, else, press keep pressing enter.

 

 

6. After this, a package.json file is created. This will have all the details , as mentioned in step 5.  We can change anytime later.

 

7. Now install Express package using command “npm install express”.

 

 

8. Once downloaded, you will notice below changes in project structure and package.json

 

9. Since the mail file is index.js, right click on project “nodebasics1”, and create a new file named as “index.js”.

 

 

 

 

 

10. Copy the code from https://www.npmjs.com/package/express and paste it in index.js.

 

 

 

11. Now execute the command “node .” [Notice the space in between node and .]

 

 12. Possibility of error here, “Port is already in use”.

 

 

If an error comes, change the port number. I am changing it to 3004 for now. Execute node . again.

 

 

13. Once executed successfully, a pop up in bottom right corner will come. Open in new tab.

 

 

 

14. To change the message or any changes, stop the server by closing the tab and executing Ctrl+C in integrated terminal. When Ctrl C is executed, it will show as ^C.

 

 15. Change the message and execute again with node . .

 

16. Open the server.

 

 

Welcome

entry.title: Re: error on using "case" instead of "if" with "Applicati...
entry.content:
The switch statement in JavaScript uses strict equality check (see https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Statements/switch#description). Therefore, the expression in the switch and case clause must be of same type. "getActivePage" returns the active page object so you need to compare with a page object not a string. From your question I can't see where e.g. "Page_KPI" comes from.

For me both options are working, see below. However, there are two constraints:

1. This sample only works in a script on page 2, because you can only access the current active page. Maybe someone else has a recommendation or trick to work around that. Unfortunately there seems to be no method to get the page name which would be one option to avoid the reference to the other page object.

2. In the switch case the page in case must be a local variable (here "other_page"). This seems to be a bug.

 

var activePage = Application.getActivePage();
var otherPage = Page_2;

if (activePage == otherPage) {
	console.log("if-yes");
} else {
	console.log("if-no");
}

switch(activePage) {
        // only works with local variables (seems to be a bug)
	case otherPage:
		console.log("switch-yes");
		break;
	default:
		console.log("switch-no");
		break;
}

entry.title: Premium Hub CoE – Workflow in SAP Transportation Management Docs
entry.content:
Many times we get this requirement, where Business want TM docs should get approval while changing of a resource, Charge Amount Changes, Removal of some items from the document.

We ( Varsha, Sanjeev ) from COE India Team have developed a simple Workflow example to cater these use cases for some customers and want to share with you so that you can also do the same.

Our Example is very simple, Freight Order will be on block status on creation and when approved, it will remove the Block from Freight Order.

When  Freight order is created, We are setting Freight Order in execution block, It will sends notification to approve to remove Freight execution block.

To achieve this once Freight Order set up is ready create workflow and trigger workflow during FO creation as explained below .

  1. Set FO Block on FO creation

 

 

 

 

 

  1. To set FO execution block -> By creating instance of /SCMTMS/CL_BLOCK_MANAGER_TOR and adding entry for execution block with block category – Manual block during FO creation
  2. Trigger Workflow -> Trigger workflow during FO creation after setting FO block.

Trigger workflow using CL_SWF_EVT_EVENT=> RAISE based on workflow event class - ZCL_ZTK_WF_TOR_APPR

 

 

 

  1. Workflow set up for approval and remove FO block

 

 

 

 

Step 1 – Decision to Remove Execution block on FO, by passing agents as user who intiates Workflow, here we can add agents role, org unit etc

 

 

Considering standard decision Task as requirement is only to approve or reject. if more information like comments to be added then create Custom task and make changes accordingly

 

 

 

 

Step 2 . Based on Decision to remove FO execution blank , remove Blocking form FO

 

Create Workflow task by passing Object type and Method which actually removes FO execution block by calling /SCMTMS/TOR Root Level action – OVERRULE_BLOCK

 

 

 

Execution

  1. Once FO is created and Saved, Execution Block is set with Block category – Manual block

 

 

 

  1. Workflow is trigger for FO which is created - 6100000750

 

 

  1. Accept the Workflow notification for FO created

 

 

  1. Freight Order Execution Block is overruled and , Execution is not blocked anymore

entry.title: NEW Concur’s Technical Document Portal
entry.content:
Hello all. I have been working as a Technical Consultant at SAP Concur for several years, and I understand the challenges our customers and partners face in finding essential technical documentation for their interface development work, which is often located in different places. To address this issue, we have created a centralized hub called Concur’s Technical Document portal.

Whether you are a seasoned veteran in Concur development or just starting out, our Technical Document Portal provides easy access to a wide range of resources, including information on SSO and SFTP connections, flat file specs and toolkits, available web services APIs, and important setup guides and FAQs for use with all aspects of your SAP ICS integration.

Probably, you won’t need all the listed resources for your Concur implementation, but the aim is that you find the main resources and starting points for your technical scope on the portal.

Key features of our Technical Document Portal include:

  1. Connecting to Concur:  Find all the information you need to establish your SFTP, SSO, or web services connections with Concur. 
  2. Interface Documentation: Dive into our in-depth technical documentation, which covers different areas of interface development including flat file integrations, web services APIs, and prebuilt SAP integrations.
  3. Best Practices: Find links to step-by-step guides, and a number of blog posts related to recent developments and best practices around your interface development work with Concur, written by our Expert Technical Consultants. 

I am confident that Concur’s Technical Document Portal can help anyone working on the technical aspects of Concur implementation.

entry.title: 1MV Cash Application Machine Learning Model - How to get 5,000-10,000 cleared payments in one Model
entry.content:
Dear Sir or Madam,

The following question has arisen when using machine learning to post-process the bank statement.

This documentation https://me.sap.com/notes/0003001671 is pointing out that 5,000 -10,000 cleared payments must be available in order to train the ML model sufficiently.

To do this, we carry out the following steps:

  1. ML_CASH_APP_JOB_STATUS_DP and set status to “In Progress” (P_INPROG)
  2. ML_CASH_APP_DATA_POST to extract training data
  3. ML_CASH_APP_JOB_STATUS_DP and set status to “Complete” (P_COMPL) -> This will trigger training in cloud

The Model Manager for SAP Cash Application can now be found in the BTP Cockpit:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_CASH_APPLICATION/9668f05979b644cd960f0d83ae4ed0aa/73fd86f7258b46bf84ef47f415af18fa.html?locale=en-US

When steps 1 to 3 are executed, a new inactive model is created. With regard to the processed items, the statistics show that not all cleared payments from the ERP are processed cumulatively. We can recognize this by the number of items when we run this training job every month. The number does not accumulate. In addition, each training run creates a new model that must then be activated.

How are we supposed to obtain an ML model that has generated 5,000-10,000 cleared payments if each training run only processes a part or new cleared payments and then generates a new model on this basis?

Who can explain this in detail?

Thank you very much
Sebastian

entry.title: Re: manage RFQ app error. Follow-on doc. is inconsistent....
entry.content:
Hello Together,

In order to use a different RFQ type  you will have to configure as well the Supplyer quotation document type.

the RFQ type must match the Supplier quotation doc type.

For EX: Standard we have RFQ doc type RQ and We want to configure a new type SA. We create a copy by RQ and assign the "Allowed item categories and Link to Purchase requisitions.

SPRO> IMG> Material Management> Purchasing > Sourcing > Define document types for RFQ.

 

Next step is to configure the corresponding Doc type for Supplier quotation.

SPRO> IMG> Material Management> Purchasing > Sourcing > Define Document Types for Supplier Quotations.

Here we create a copy by RQ and give the same type as above in our case SA and after we are configuring the follow on doc categories and types.

Next step is to allow the Document categories and types:

SPRO> IMG> Material Management> Purchasing > Sourcing > Blockchain Enablement > Allowed Document Categories and Types.

Here we create a new entry for doc category R and Doc type SA.

 

Now you will be able to see the follow on documents for the RFQ type SA in Manage RFQ app and create supplier quotations.

 

Regards,

Teo.

entry.title: Distribution based on dynamic weights using #Variablemembers in SAC
entry.content:
I am trying to distribute some values using a data action, in this example of Plan 2023 from an unassigned member, to the members of Product A and Product B of Product Dimension. I want the distribution to be based on the weight of Products of the Actuals of 2023. For simplicity I differentiate the Actuals and Plan using a dimension called "value type" (Actuals have the value 010 and Plan the 020).

I tried applying the following code:

//Define the membersets

MEMBERSET [d/Product]=("Product A","Product B")

MEMBERSET [d/Date].[p/YEAR]="2023"

MEMBERSET [d/Company_Code]="Company A"

MEMBERSET [d/Value_Type]=("020")

//Define variables

VARIABLEMEMBER #Weight OF [d/Product]

VARIABLEMEMBER #SumProduct OF [d/Product]

// Step 1: Calculate Total Actuals for 2023

DATA([d/Product]=#SumProduct)=RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]="Measure",

   [d/Version]="public.Actual",

   [d/Value_Type]="010",

   [d/Date]="2023",

   [d/Company_Code]="Company A")

//Step 2: Calculate Distribution Weights

FOREACH [d/Product]

DATA([d/Measures]="Measure", [d/Product]=#Weight)=RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]="Measure",

   [d/Version]="public.Actual",

   [d/Value_Type]="010",

   [d/Date]="2023",

   [d/Company_Code]="Company A")

/ RESULTLOOKUP([d/Product]=#SumProduct)                                

ENDFOR

//Step 3: Distribute the values

DATA()=RESULTLOOKUP([d/Product]="#",

[d/Version]="public.Actual",

[d/Value_Type]="020",

[d/Date]="2023",

[d/Company_Code]="Company A")

* RESULTLOOKUP([d/Product]=#Weight)

//Step 4: Delete the data from "Unassigned" after the distribution

DATA([d/Date]="2023",[d/Product]="#",[d/Value_Type]="020")=0

Do you have any suggestions?

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: Problem when binding CAP application with PostgreSQL,...
entry.content:
Hi Edoardo,

I'm not quite sure if you can change the db but we did manage to re-use a single db in multiple CAP applications by specifying the schema in the pool configuration. You can potentially change the db in there as well?

We specify the schema in the package.json "cds" config as follows:

"requires": {
  "db": {
    "credentials": {
      "schema": "yourschemaname"
    }
  }
}

Note: cds.requires.db has a "pool" property as well, you could check that too.

Best
Martin

entry.title: Re: 1MV Cash Application Machine Learning Model - How to...
entry.content:
Hi Sebastian,

 

I think there is a huge misunderstanding. You should not retrain your model each month. Usually you should wait until you reached a certain size, above the limitation of an AI model, then train your model once and then you are good to go for the future. Depending on how much changes you have in your business, did you aquire new customers who do not follow your pattern of providing the information in the memo line as you need it, then you have to do a retraining again.

But in general, the idea is not to retrain each week or month. But rather try to keep it stable.

 

Best,

Edrilan Berisha

entry.title: Quality inspection before Ship notice
entry.content:
Dear Experts , 

We are implementing Quality inspection before ship notice in the system , We have followed all the steps instructed by sap Ariba for the same , the configuartion performed in sap system are 

1) Maintained the Creation of Event type linkage for Inspection lot.

2) Maintaied the change Event type linkage for inspection lot.

3)Maintained event type linkage for usage decision.

4)Maintained outbound parameter for Outbound transactions.

I am not able to generated Quality inspection request in proxy (SRT_moni) , I have put a breakout for the function module ARBCIG_EXT_EVENT_INSP_LOT_CRTD , which is responsible for creating the proxy , but it is not hitting the breakpoint. 

My thought process is , i might be missing a Output configuration as for all other document type( PO, GR , SES ,ERP SES) there is a Output process ( nace) but cannot see any Nace Configuration for Quality inspection, please let me know how i can proceed here.

Note: I am using Qi07 to generate the Quality Inspection lot , is this the correct T-code or is there anyother T-code to generate the same 

Thanks & regards

Rithvik. R

entry.title: Display Change Logs - Sales Document
entry.content:
To enhance the customer experience in monitoring changes to sales documents, the Display Change Logs – Sales Documents app (F7356) is released in SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2408 to offer an overview of sales document change logs. This app integrates various types of sales document change logs. You can apply filters to view specific change logs or display all change logs directly in their system. The app also offers user-friendly change descriptions and allows you to view and analyze sales document change logs from multiple perspectives.
Prior to the introduction of this application, you may have used the "display document changes" feature to view the change logs of a specific document. However, the resulting information looks technical.

 The object page of the Manage Sales Orders - Version 2, Manage Sales Quotations - Version 2, or Manage Sales Contracts - Version 2 app also has a "Display Change Log" button. However, the button only allows you to view the change logs of a specific document.

 

App Overview

You can access the app with business role SAP_BR_INTERNAL_SALES_REP or SAP_BR_SALES_MANAGER. The app supports change types such as insertion, deletion, and modification. You can apply filters or use fuzzy search to find specific change logs that you are interested in. Each log entry contains information about a change made to a document header, item, or schedule line. You can view details such as the changed value, the person who made the change, the time of the change, and more.

 

Change Description

The app displays a change description for each log entry. You can easily tell what fields or table entries were changed from the change descriptions. So, get ready to experience a whole new level of convenience as you navigate through your change logs.

 

Comprehensive Log Details

Each log entry contains much information. In addition to the old and new field values, the log entry also includes details from sales documents. These include the sales area, sold-to party, solution order, customer reference, and sales document type. Just click on the settings icon to select the fields you want to view.

 

Filters and Fuzzy Search

This app offers an array of filters. In addition to the standard filters such as change type, changed by, and changed on, the app also offers additional filters that pertain to sales documents and allow you to further refine your search. These include the sales area, sold to party, solution order, customer reference, and sales document type. With just a click of the "adapt filters" button, you can easily select the filters that best suit your needs. You can also perform a fuzzy search by entering a document number or other attributes into the search field.

 

Authorization Control

You can maintain restrictions on the sales area, sales document type, and price element access level for relevant business roles. It helps you build different roles with different authorization requirements. For example, as for price element authorization control, the administrator can define an access level for each condition type. If your role has no read/write access to certain condition types, you may notice certain information missing in the related change logs. In the following screenshot, the entry logs a recent update to the condition type "Manual Price" in sales document 548200. Data such as the field, change description, and old and new values is missing because the user is not authorized to view such data.

entry.title: New SAP Analytics Cloud Performance Workshop
entry.content:
Attention, SAP Analytics Cloud professionals!  

 Get ready for a new SAP Analytics Cloud performance training course.  

 Boost your skills in SAP backend modeling and SAP Analytics Cloud performance story design with our specialized training course, “Backend Modeling with SAP Solutions and SAP Analytics Cloud Story Performance Optimization. “ 

This one-day training course is designed for seasoned SAP Analytics Cloud users who want to explore SAP`s strategic data modeling options and discover the relationship between story design and performance in SAP Analytics Cloud.  

You can expect to acquire basic knowledge of data modeling in SAP's central solutions, such as SAP BW/4HANA, SAP Datasphere, or SAP HANA. You will also apply story design best practices and performance analysis techniques to improve your story performance in SAP Analytics Cloud.  

 Are you curious? Book your seats for US or EMEA timezone here

This means that 10,000 is also an optimum and the results will not improve significantly with another 10,000 very similar data sets?

entry.title: Re: Drop Down not working in Mobile App - SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
Hi All,

As we are using an existing OData from our backend SAP system, the issue is with the entityset mapped to the dropdown field.
GET ENTITYSET method adds a blank record(need to go back and check why this was done) while returning values. This blank record is the root cause. In the web preview the blank entry can be seen in the drop down, but in mobile preview this blank entry is not visible and also interferes with selection.

Just putting it out there for discussion and if there is a patch required from SAP as the same component is behaving differently on web vs mobile preview.

entry.title: Automatic purge of performance forms
entry.content: We are looking for an automatic way to purge user performance forms with X years for Active users and Y years for Inactive users. Setting the DTRM times is easy through data management. But, how can we achieve an automatic purge? Manual purge involves several manual steps. System Identifier purge is not fit for purpose. Auto Data Purge doesn't allow us the granular choice of only purging the performance agreement forms. How can we automatically purge the performance forms based on the defined DTRM times?

entry.content:
Hi,

Please check T-code：BUSB, and check for the presence of an entry for BUS000FLDS.

entry.title: Re: Migration tool connection to SAP PO
entry.content:
Hi Ramu,

Looks like the User ID that is being used to connect ID and ESR doesn't have proper roles assigned.

Check if the user is assigned with the below roles:

  • SAP_XI_API_DISPLAY_J2EE
  • SAP_XI_API_DEVELOP_J2EE
  • SAP_XI_MONITOR_J2EE

Thanks

Sai

entry.title: What happens with SDI virtual tables and remote sources when DPAgent went offline?
entry.content:
Hello,

first of all I would like to describe the situation:

We have BW/4 Hana installed and with the help of SDI and DPAgent tables from MS SQL Server are present in SAP BW/4 schema (SAPHANADB). 

This is my question:

Because of security updates host os of DPAgent needs a reboot.

  • What happens in case DPAgent is down with the created virtual tabels, remote sources and datasources build up of them?
  • Do BW/4 processes still have access to the these tables, is there a kind of caching available? 
  • What is best practice updating the DPAgent concerning the connected systems? Should BW/4 have a downtime also?
  • What are your experiences?

I'm looking forward to your answers!

Best regards,

Sascha

Try to remove POD-relevant settings?

entry.title: Customer Checkout Loyalty issue
entry.content:
Hello,

I configured a loyalty rule: ie If an item having discount, points will not be accumilated.

(rule image attached) It is working fine.

But if the same invoice having another item without discount the loyalty points calculated for both the discounted and the non discounted item. How to resolve this issue? Is there any other rule to create?

Also want to know, how to achive different point calculation for each tier. (If silver: point calculated as 1 to 1.25, if gold: point calculated as 1 to 1.5, for platinum: point calculated as 1 to 2)

Please help me to sort this out.

entry.title: Re: BI Commentary Application - DB and schema?

I see the Commentary Service in one of my APS servers

But when I try to Save a comment, nothing happens. I never see that comment again.

I imagine I have to activate these checks to make the store of comments available, right?

Thanks, best regards

Victor

entry.title: Re: What is your Favorite Holiday Movie?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I know some will say Die Hard, It's a Wonderful Life, or maybe you're like my brother and love Chevy Chase's Christmas Vacation... but for me, it is Home Alone. The way Kevin outsmarts the burglars while his family is away feels like an epic castle defense app. It's like playing song at Castle APK, where you strategize to protect your stronghold. Every trap Kevin sets feels like placing another defense in the app, and it’s always thrilling to see how it all plays out. It's a perfect blend of holiday cheer and clever strategy, making it my go-to movie every year.

Thank!

entry.title: Re: How to set copy rule for condition in VK11
entry.content: Hello Henry, this answer was the correct one. Perfect. Thank you so much 🙂

entry.title: Re: What is the difference between these two offerings fr...
entry.content:
Hello @ChandraV ,

The content for both courses is the same. The only difference is that the paid version is eight days of  3-hour per day instructor-led virtual training, while the free version is self-paced. 

You won't need to have EC implemented for both courses. You are given a demo environment for the paid version as part of the training. For self-paced, you can subscribe to a practice environment to use for the exercises included in the course. 

The only prerequisite for Employee Central is to complete the THR80 SuccessFactors Platform course.  (https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/explore-the-sap-successfactors-platform).

Hope this helps.

entry.title: Purchase expenses (LANDED COST) are borne by the buyer and paid in cash. How do I deal?
entry.content:
HELLO EVERYONE  I wish you a nice day ..

I have this scenario how to do it inside SAP B1  If you use freight, the supplier’s balance will become (the value of the goods + the shipping value). Correct? Sometimes when purchasing goods, I am the one who pays the shipping costs in cash ( for example, to the driver who brings the materials. How do I deal?) (the supplier’s account is not affected. His balance remains only the value of the goods).So I think it is more correct to use LANDED COST . It is assumed that I have two accounts affected in addition to the inventory account, which are (the transportation expense account and the cash account). How do I handle this scenario and apply it in SAP B1?

let us take an example that I applied in SAP B1, which is that I created a GRPO document with a value of 150,000, so the journal entry was like this:

Dr:inventory ACC

Cr. GRNI ACC

NOW I bears the transportation cost for these goods, and I paid them directly in cash. The transportation expenses were 10,000. The journal entry appeared like this when creating a landing cost document:

Dr:inventory ACC

Cr:Transportation Expenses ACC

In this case, firstly, the cash accounts did not move with a value of 10,000, and also in the expenses account a credit appeared. Isn’t a Debited supposed to appear and the cash accounts move? I'm sure there's a mistake I made, but I don't know where. If so, can you explain how to handle this scenario within SAP B1? Thank you

entry.title: Quality system | SU25 - Initially fill the Customer Table
entry.content:
Dear Team,

In our Quality system, we have implemented Fiori and we need to execute 'Initially fill the Customer Tables' in SU25 tcode as part rapid activation --> Is that mandatory to open the client to execute this as part rapid activation or we can make it through TR movement?

Regards,

Basis Team.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Quality system | SU25 - Initially fill the Customer T...
entry.content:
Dear Basis team,

 

as documented, the standard approach for Rapid activation is to perform the tasks in DEV and then transport the settings to QAS/PRD.

If you are using a different approach and perform changes directly in QAS, you are responsible to set the system and client change settings accordingly.


Also for SU25 the same recommendation exists, to perform all settings in DEV and then transport to QAS for testing and later, after tests have been finished successfully transport the final settings to PRD.

If you decide, to have different SU25 data in your systems within the same transport landscape, this is your decision and will require future attention for all upcoming changes regarding authorization maintenance tasks.

Therefore please discuss the best approach for your scenario with your consultant, to find the best way for your specific requirements.

Technically your plan is possible, but bears some risks, which you need to pay attention to.

Hope, this helps,

b.rgds, Bernhard

entry.title: Re: Distribution based on dynamic weights using #Variable...
entry.content:
@dionysis_evripidou 

Assuming other dimensions of the model are same in master data for Actual and Plan values. Something like below should work.

MEMBERSET [d/Product]=("Product A","Product B")
MEMBERSET [d/Date] = BASEMEMBER([d/Date].[h/YQM], "2023")
MEMBERSET [d/Company_Code]="Company A"
MEMBERSET [d/Value_Type]=("020")
MEMBERSET [d/Measures]="Measure"

//Define variables

VARIABLEMEMBER #Weight OF [d/Product]

VARIABLEMEMBER #SumProduct OF [d/Product]

// Step 1: Calculate Total Actuals for 2023

DATA([d/Product]=#SumProduct)=RESULTLOOKUP([d/Version]="public.Actual",[d/Value_Type]="010")

 

//Step 2: Distribute the values

DATA()=RESULTLOOKUP([d/Version]="public.Actual",[d/Value_Type]="010")/ RESULTLOOKUP([d/Product]=#SumProduct)* RESULTLOOKUP([d/Product]="#",[d/Value_Type]="020")

//Step 4: Delete the data from "Unassigned" after the distribution

DELETE([d/Product]="#",[d/Value_Type]="020")

 

Nikhil

entry.title: insufficient privilege currency conversion to shared spaces
entry.content:
Hi 

I just want to confirm I'm not doing anything wrong and there is no workaround havng to build out any currency conversions in the 'end' Space raher than a 'shared' view from another space.

I am getting an 'insufficient privilege' error on the TCUR* tables when I use them in a Space where the DB user is assigned to that Space. I have shared the TCUR* tables with that Space, I have also tried doing the conversion in the previous space so it just carries through the 'calculated' fields but both attempts fail with this error.

The only method I have working is to share TCUR* tables with Space, generate TCUR* views of that table in the Space and then create the conversion field in my view in that Space. 

I have a 4 layer architecture (Inbound, Harmonisation, Propogation and Reporting). Propogation is in one Space and Reporting in many spaces (depending on user area). Ideally I would like to do the Currency Conversion in Propogation Space then share that view out to Other Reporting Spaces. Currently I have to recreate all Currency Conversions individually in all reporting Spaces along with ceating views of all TCUR tables in each reporting space.

Can someone confirm that there is no way around this? Seems a little mad, you can share views/tables from another space and DB user can see anything shared, why not currency tables and currency conversion calculations with a shared view?

 

Any help, most appreciated

entry.title: Re: How to search for only one property in Object Table o...
entry.content:
Hello @Jitendra_Kansal, The issue regarding productID is also resolved. Thanks for your suggestions and help

 let pageClientData =context.binding;
    let productId = pageClientData.productId;

    // Simple value assignment.
    let search = context.searchString;
    let qoB = context.dataQueryBuilder();
  let appFilter =`ProductId eq '${productId}'`;
    if (!search) {
       qoB.filter(appFilter);      
    }

    if(search && search != ''){
        let username = qoB.filterTerm(`substringof(tolower('${search}'),tolower(Name))`);
       
        qoB.filter(appFilter).and(username);

    }
    return qoB;

entry.title: AP posting cannot get payment terms automatically from supplier master data
entry.content:
I used migration object "FI - Accounts payable open item" to migrate my AP open items data, the problem was that no payment terms on the journal entry items generated, payment term should be picked up automatically from supplier master data because it worked when I tested it with MIRO app in foreground.

 

Error sample by migration template: JE DIGITS (company code US01)

 

Correct sample by MIRO manually: JE PHONE_NUMBER (company code US01)

 

screenshots attached.

entry.title: SAP Cloud ALM Health Monitoring short dump metrics (e.g. last 5 minutes)
entry.content:
Hi

We use SAP Cloud ALM for monitoring our SAP productive systems.
Unfortunately, we are missing the following functions in Health Monitoring, which are available in SAP Solution Manager:
- Number of short dumps (last hour)
- Number of short dumps (last 5 minutes)

Will this be implemented soon?

Thank you!


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM Health Monitoring short dump metrics (e...
entry.content:
Currently SAP Cloud ALM Health Monitoring offers only the metric "Short Dumps today" for on-premise ABAP based systems. It has a single event that can be configured for generating alerts, e-mails, etc.: "High Number of Short Dumps"

The public roadmap for SAP Cloud ALM does not currently have any entries related to Health Monitoring metrics related to short dumps.
No new developments are planned at this time. 


If you desire more Health Monitoring metrics or events related to short dumps, you will need access the relevant SAP Customer Influence campaign related to SAP Cloud ALM for Operations. 
The full list of campaigns can be found in KBA 3391676.

entry.title: Re: SMTP Server setup / XS855 error message
entry.content: It seems you're encountering issues with your current SMTP server setup (Host: 192.168.3.3, Port: 25). While troubleshooting, you might want to consider alternative SMTP server options like Digitalaka™ or SendGrid, which offer reliable email delivery services with robust features and support.
